project_reference.core_research_question: Does DSSYK have a semiclassical dS bulk dual?
project_reference.current_focus: Literature-based assessment of semiclassical de Sitter bulk dual proposals for double-scaled SYK

scope.question: Does existing primary-source evidence support a bona fide semiclassical de Sitter bulk dual for DSSYK, or only a narrower sector-specific correspondence?
in_scope: Assess primary-source evidence for a semiclassical de Sitter bulk dual of DSSYK through April 9, 2026
Distinguish doubled constrained infinite-temperature sectors from claims about full DSSYK
out_of_scope: Constructing a new bulk dual from first principles
Surveying unrelated de Sitter holography models not directly tied to DSSYK
unresolved_questions: Is the best-supported bulk description genuinely 3d de Sitter gravity or a lower-dimensional periodic/sine-dilaton toy model?
Do entropy and Hilbert-space puzzles rule out only the naive interpretation or any semiclassical dS dual?

user_asserted_anchors: Doubled DSSYK correlator matching is not enough by itself
Entropy and Hilbert-space structure must remain visible in the verdict
known_good_baselines: Later periodic-dilaton reproductions of DSSYK correlators
context_gaps: Need a precise boundary between sector-specific evidence and a full bulk-dual claim
crucial_inputs: Primary-source arXiv metadata and current INSPIRE-linked publication records checked on April 9, 2026

formulations: Primary-source literature synthesis focused on correlators, semiclassical control, entropy, temperature, and Hilbert-space structure
allowed_estimator_families: Direct comparison of published correlator and thermodynamic results
forbidden_fit_families: Ignoring negative evidence from entropy or Hilbert-space analyses
stop_and_rethink_conditions: If support for a semiclassical dS dual rests only on two-point-function matching while later papers contradict the thermodynamic or state-space interpretation

observables.name: DSSYK-to-dS correlator matching
observables.definition: Whether the doubled DSSYK two-point function matches a de Sitter bulk scalar Green's function in the claimed semiclassical regime

observables.name: Semiclassical control regime
observables.definition: Whether the literature identifies a controllable small-coupling or large-N regime with suppressed bulk fluctuations

observables.name: Entropy and Hilbert-space compatibility
observables.definition: Whether entropy, temperature, and Hilbert-space results remain compatible with a semiclassical de Sitter bulk interpretation

claims.statement: Current evidence supports a constrained, sector-specific semiclassical de Sitter interpretation for DSSYK observables, but does not yet establish a complete semiclassical dS bulk dual for DSSYK as a whole.

deliverables.description: Corrected manuscript-style report stating the verdict on whether DSSYK has a semiclassical dS bulk dual
Journal of GPD command outputs, failures, and research reasoning
must_contain: analysis of what GPD caught or missed

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check that the report accurately records the positive correlator and semiclassical-control evidence from the must-read references
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All major positive evidence from the approved references appears with the correct scope

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check that the report explicitly incorporates entropy, temperature, and Hilbert-space objections rather than bypassing them
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Obstruction evidence is integrated into the verdict and not relegated to a footnote

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Review whether the final verdict distinguishes a sector-specific dual from a claim about full DSSYK
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The verdict makes the scope distinction explicit and avoids overclaiming

references.locator: V. Narovlansky and H. Verlinde, arXiv:2310.16994 / JHEP 05 (2025) 032
references.why_it_matters: Primary positive claim that doubled DSSYK reproduces de Sitter correlators and suggests a JT/dS bulk dual

references.locator: H. Verlinde and M. Zhang, arXiv:2402.02584 / JHEP 05 (2025) 053
references.why_it_matters: Establishes exact all-orders two-point-function agreement between DSSYK and 2D Liouville-de Sitter gravity.

references.locator: A. Blommaert, T.G. Mertens and J. Papalini, arXiv:2404.03535 / JHEP 06 (2025) 050
references.why_it_matters: Provides the strongest periodic/sine-dilaton bulk realization reproducing q-Schwarzian DSSYK structure
Shows that upper-edge spectral scaling of DSSYK reproduces de Sitter JT gravity and links that limit to sine-dilaton behavior.

references.locator: A.A. Rahman and L. Susskind, arXiv:2312.04097
references.why_it_matters: Makes the entropy/area disagreement assumption-sensitive and therefore prevents the positive evidence from being read too naively.

references.locator: A. Blommaert et al., arXiv:2411.16922 / JHEP 07 (2025) 093
references.why_it_matters: Identifies entropy and finite-dimensional Hilbert-space consequences that pressure a naive semiclassical de Sitter interpretation

references.locator: C. Cui and M. Rozali, arXiv:2509.01680 / JHEP 02 (2026) 160
references.why_it_matters: Latest positive evidence that bulk matter correlators and wormhole Hilbert-space structures reproduce DSSYK correlators

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-main
forbidden_proxies.reason: Would bypass thermodynamic and Hilbert-space consistency checks that are essential to a full semiclassical bulk-dual claim

weakest_anchors: Whether periodic/sine-dilaton gravity should count as semiclassical de Sitter gravity in the same sense as the original doubled-DSSYK proposal
unvalidated_assumptions: That the constrained doubled infinite-temperature sector diagnoses the bulk dual of full DSSYK
competing_explanations: DSSYK may exhibit semiclassical geometry only for restricted observables while its full state space remains too discrete for a standard semiclassical de Sitter bulk
disconfirming_observations: Entropy and Hilbert-space analyses remain irreconcilable with semiclassical de Sitter expectations even after the newest bulk reconstructions

position.current_phase_name: Map the primary literature and competing bulk-duality claims
position.last_activity: 2026-04-09: verified citations against arXiv/INSPIRE-linked records, audited retained equations, and rewrote the manuscript

intermediate_results.description: Narovlansky-Verlinde: in a doubled infinite-temperature DSSYK sector with an equal-energy constraint, dressed two-point functions match a scalar Green function in dS_3 and motivate a JT/dS-style bulk interpretation.
intermediate_results.validity: large N; doubled sector; constrained dressed operators; radius restored explicitly for dimensional clarity

intermediate_results.description: Verlinde-Zhang: a 2D Liouville-de Sitter gravity model reproduces the exact DSSYK two-point function to all orders in the double-scaling parameter and is argued to arise from quantizing 3D de Sitter gravity.
intermediate_results.validity: normalized two-point functions; doubled sector; all orders in lambda = p^2/N

intermediate_results.description: Blommaert-Mertens-Papalini: sine-dilaton gravity provides a precise holographic dual of DSSYK at the q-Schwarzian level, reproduces thermodynamics with the positivity constraint, and implies a discrete bulk Hilbert space.
intermediate_results.validity: q-Schwarzian / thermodynamic sector; semi-classical sine-dilaton regime

intermediate_results.description: Okuyama: a scaling limit near the upper edge of the DSSYK spectrum reproduces de Sitter JT gravity and is consistent with the classical behavior of sine-dilaton gravity.
intermediate_results.validity: upper-edge spectral scaling limit of DSSYK

intermediate_results.description: Cui-Rozali: splitting and gluing in sine-dilaton gravity reproduces DSSYK matter correlators, including OTOCs and double-trumpet observables, and builds a wormhole Hilbert-space picture.
intermediate_results.validity: bulk matter sector in sine-dilaton gravity; chord-diagram observables

intermediate_results.description: Blommaert et al.: periodic dilaton gravity and DSSYK exhibit an entropic puzzle; gauging a discrete shift symmetry discretizes lengths, leads to a finite-dimensional Hilbert space, and spoils a naive Bekenstein-Hawking interpretation.
intermediate_results.validity: periodic/sine-dilaton realization; entropy and state-counting sector

intermediate_results.description: Current literature supports a restricted, sector-specific semiclassical de Sitter interpretation for parts of DSSYK, but not a settled complete semiclassical dS bulk dual for DSSYK as a whole.
intermediate_results.validity: assessment based on correlator, thermodynamic, and Hilbert-space evidence across restricted sectors

open_questions: Does the upper-edge dS JT scaling limit describe the same bulk physics as the doubled DSSYK construction, or only a related limit of the model?

decisions.summary: Use the local CLI surface rather than the unavailable runtime-only new-project command
decisions.rationale: The workspace prompt lists GPD commands, but the installed local CLI exposes init/state/phase/result workflows rather than a direct new-project mutation command; proceeding with the available commands preserves auditability and documents the gap.

decisions.summary: Adopt a restricted-sector verdict rather than a full semiclassical-bulk-dual claim
decisions.rationale: Primary-source evidence from 2023-2026 supports correlator and low-dimensional bulk reconstructions for specific DSSYK sectors and limits, but entropy and Hilbert-space objections prevent a clean claim that full DSSYK already has a settled semiclassical de Sitter bulk dual.

approximations.name: Large-N semiclassical limit
approximations.validity_range: Large N with suppressed 1/N fluctuations in the proposed bulk regime
approximations.current_value: formal large-N limit in cited papers

approximations.name: Double-scaling limit
approximations.validity_range: DSSYK with q and N scaled so lambda stays finite; semiclassical claims strongest at small lambda or edge scaling limits
approximations.controlling_param: lambda = p^2/N (or equivalent fixed double-scaling parameter)
approximations.current_value: finite lambda; small-lambda and edge-of-spectrum limits emphasized

approximations.name: Doubled constrained infinite-temperature sector
approximations.validity_range: Applies to the doubled DSSYK construction with equal-energy or constrained-sector dressing used in de Sitter proposals
approximations.current_value: central in Narovlansky-Verlinde and related doubled constructions

convention_lock.metric_signature: mostly-plus (-,+,...) Lorentzian signature for de Sitter/JT bulk formulas
convention_lock.coordinate_system: Global dS2/dS3 time with static-patch interpretation when discussing horizon thermodynamics

propagated_uncertainties.quantity: Scope of the bulk dual (restricted sector vs full DSSYK)
propagated_uncertainties.method: primary-source synthesis across positive and critical papers
compare Narovlansky-Verlinde assumptions with Rahman-Susskind comments and later periodic-dilaton entropy analysis

propagated_uncertainties.quantity: Whether sine-dilaton gravity should count as de Sitter gravity for this duality claim
propagated_uncertainties.method: compare the 2025-2026 bulk constructions with the original dS3/JT interpretations
